note: Image Field Caption extends the core image field (re-classing its field item) to add an optional rich-text caption entered per image, displayed via an 'Image with caption' formatter, with caption text kept in standalone DB tables rather than the field columns. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) probing documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On Drupal 11 I want editors to add a caption under each image on an existing image field. Using the Image Field Caption module, what do I turn on in field settings and which display formatter do I choose?
evals.expected_output: In the image field's settings enable the 'Enable Caption field' checkbox (setting key caption_field). Then on Manage display set the field's format to 'Image with caption' (formatter plugin id image_caption). Optionally set caption_field_required to make it mandatory.

evals.prompt: Where is the global admin settings page for the Image Field Caption module, and does it add any permissions or Drush commands?
evals.expected_output: There is no global settings page — the module has no configure route. Configuration is per image field (field settings + Manage display formatter). It provides no permissions, no Drush commands, and no config schema.

evals.prompt: In Image Field Caption, where is the caption text stored — in the image field's own columns or elsewhere? Name the storage.
evals.expected_output: Captions are stored in standalone database tables (image_field_caption and image_field_caption_revision), not in the field columns, via the image_field_caption.storage service (ImageCaptionStorage), keyed by entity_type, bundle, field_name, entity_id, revision_id, language and delta.

evals.prompt: How does Image Field Caption implement its display — does it define a new field type? Name the formatter plugin id and what it extends.
evals.expected_output: It does not define a new field type. It re-classes the core image field item to ImageCaptionItem via hook_field_info_alter, and provides a formatter plugin id 'image_caption' (ImageCaptionFormatter extends core ImageFormatter) that swaps the theme hook to image_caption_formatter.

evals.prompt: I want to change the HTML wrapper and CSS around an Image Field Caption caption. Which Twig template do I override and what element/class wraps the caption by default?
evals.expected_output: Override templates/image-caption-formatter.html.twig in your theme (then clear caches). By default the caption is wrapped in <blockquote class="image-field-caption">, which you can also target with the CSS selector blockquote.image-field-caption.

evals.prompt: From custom code, how do I read a stored caption for a given entity/field/delta in Image Field Caption? Name the service and method.
evals.expected_output: Use the image_field_caption.storage service and call getCaption($entity_type, $bundle, $field_name, $entity_id, $revision_id, $language, $delta), which returns ['caption' => ..., 'caption_format' => ...] or an empty array.

evals.prompt: Does Image Field Caption keep a separate caption per entity revision?
evals.expected_output: Not effectively. An image_field_caption_revision table exists but the module's revision insert/delete logic is commented out, so captions are stored/loaded for the current revision only.
